source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
As shown in the figure, two parallel long straight metal rails are fixed in a horizontal plane with a distance of $l$ between them. Two identical conductor rods AB and CD are placed on the rails perpendicularly, both with a length of $l$. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the rods and the rails is $\mu$, where the maximum static friction equals the kinetic friction. The entire setup is in a uniform magnetic field with a magnetic flux density of magnitude $B$ directed vertically downward. Starting at $t=0$, an external force is applied to rod AB, causing it to undergo uniform acceleration from rest towards the right, until the external force is removed at time $t=t_{1}$. At this time, the induced current in the rod is $i_{1}$. It is known that rod CD starts to move at time $t=t_{0}$ $(0<t_{0}<t_{1})$, and both rods maintain good contact with the rails throughout the motion. The mass of both rods is $m$, and their resistance is $R$. The resistance of the rails is negligible. The magnitude of gravitational acceleration is $g$.

1. Determine the magnitude of the acceleration of rod AB during its uniformly accelerated motion.
2. Calculate the speed of rod CD when the external force is removed.
3. After the external force is removed, rod CD comes to rest at time $t=t_{2}$. Determine the magnitude of the velocity of rod AB at this instant.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly begins with 'As shown in the figure,' indicating diagram grounding
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false in the input fields
the setup involves spatial arrangement of rails, rods AB/CD, and magnetic-field orientation, which is typically diagram-supported even though much of the geometry is also described in text
asset_registry_record shows image_manifest with an existing primary image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: the problem has three sub-questions that depend on different time intervals (t0, t1, t2) and changing motion regimes
solving requires combining electromagnetic induction, magnetic force, friction, and kinematics rather than a single direct lookup
question_text includes conditions linking induced current i1, the onset of motion of rod CD at t0, and post-force dynamics after t1, indicating chained reasoning
the long question length (question_char_length=1364) is consistent with a multi-condition derivation task
verifiability: answer_text gives three explicit symbolic expressions, one for each sub-question, which are concrete targets for checking
the outputs are algebraic formulas in stated variables, making downstream symbolic or rule-based verification feasible
asset_registry_record has registry_passed=true, so the core assets needed for checking are available

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question, answer, and referenced image are all present, and registry_passed=true with no recorded issues.
Initial scoring is strong across image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability, indicating high candidate value.

Although the problem explicitly depends on the diagram and has symbolic/sign-convention checking risks, the needed supporting assets are available, so these are follow-up risks rather than grounds to lower priority.

normalized_value.rewritten_question_text:
As shown in the figure, two parallel long straight metal rails are fixed in a horizontal plane with a distance of $l$ between them. Two identical conductor rods AB and CD are placed on the rails perpendicularly, both with a length of $l$. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the rods and the rails is $\mu$, where the maximum static friction equals the kinetic friction. The entire setup is in a uniform magnetic field with a magnetic flux density of magnitude $B$ directed vertically downward. Starting at $t=0$, an external force is applied to rod AB, causing it to undergo uniform acceleration from rest towards the right, until the external force is removed at time $t=t_{1}$. At this time, the induced current in the rod is $i_{1}$. It is known that rod CD starts to move at time $t=t_{0}$ $(0<t_{0}<t_{1})$, and both rods maintain good contact with the rails throughout the motion. The mass of both rods is $m$, and their resistance is $R$. The resistance of the rails is negligible. The magnitude of gravitational acceleration is $g$.

1. Determine the magnitude of the acceleration of rod AB during its uniformly accelerated motion.
2. Calculate the speed of rod CD when the external force is removed.
3. After the external force is removed, rod CD comes to rest at time $t=t_{2}$. Determine the magnitude of the velocity of rod AB at this instant.

field_audit_records.after_value:
As shown in the figure, two parallel long straight metal rails are fixed in a horizontal plane with a distance of $l$ between them. Two identical conductor rods AB and CD are placed on the rails perpendicularly, both with a length of $l$. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the rods and the rails is $\mu$, where the maximum static friction equals the kinetic friction. The entire setup is in a uniform magnetic field with a magnetic flux density of magnitude $B$ directed vertically downward. Starting at $t=0$, an external force is applied to rod AB, causing it to undergo uniform acceleration from rest towards the right, until the external force is removed at time $t=t_{1}$. At this time, the induced current in the rod is $i_{1}$. It is known that rod CD starts to move at time $t=t_{0}$ $(0<t_{0}<t_{1})$, and both rods maintain good contact with the rails throughout the motion. The mass of both rods is $m$, and their resistance is $R$. The resistance of the rails is negligible. The magnitude of gravitational acceleration is $g$.

1. Determine the magnitude of the acceleration of rod AB during its uniformly accelerated motion.
2. Calculate the speed of rod CD when the external force is removed.
3. After the external force is removed, rod CD comes to rest at time $t=t_{2}$. Determine the magnitude of the velocity of rod AB at this instant.

variants.title: PhysReason 开放题
